finding: scopeFootnoteId reduces both a single-prefixed href-derived id (user-content-fn-1) and the double-prefixed li id (user-content-user-content-fn-1) to the identical `${contentId}-fn-1`, so the ref href scoped value === the definition li id — getElementById resolves. Verified against the removed inline logic; single-prefix output is byte-identical (no regression).

Regex handles fn/fnref kinds, custom labels (fn-note), multiply-referenced suffixes (fnref-1-2), and 0/1/2+ user-content- prefixes; `fnfn-1` / non-footnote ids fall through unchanged (no false rewrite).

a/li/h2 override outputs are a superset of the old behavior: plain heading hash, external links, and single-prefix footnotes all produce the same scoped values as before; h2 isFootnoteLabel suppresses the same labels PLUS the double-prefixed one (fixes the leaked sr-only Footnotes heading).
Pure functions, no throw paths; undefined/empty inputs guarded (`if (!id) return id`, `if (!href) return href`).

scopeHref never converts a hash into a non-# scheme: it returns either the original href (external, unchanged — same vector surface as before, still routed through the override's target=_blank rel=noreferrer) or a `#...` fragment. No new javascript:/data: vector introduced.

Re-scoping past the sanitize `user-content-` clobber guard is safe: the emitted id is ALWAYS `${contentId}-fn-/-fnref-<suffix>` (contentId is a server content UUID), so an LLM-controlled footnote label can never yield a bare clobbering id (e.g. `location`/`constructor`) — strictly safer than a bare name.

`^(?:user-content-)*(fn|fnref)-(.+)$` has no overlapping/nested quantifiers; the repeated group is a fixed literal and `.+` is a single greedy run — linear time, no catastrophic backtracking on adversarial `user-content-`×N input.
Helper signatures (string|undefined -> string|undefined) match the component call sites; tsc --noEmit clean.
Mirrors the imageSrcPolicy.ts pure-module + colocated node:test precedent already cited in useStreamdownComponents.tsx; reuses slugifyHeading/safeDecode rather than duplicating slug logic.
Export names scopeFootnoteId/scopeHref/isFootnoteLabel are verb-first and consistent with surrounding chat/utils naming; comments carry the double-prefix rationale.

Footnote ref stays a focusable <a href>; Enter/click both activate via the unchanged onClick. Suppressing the redundant sr-only Footnotes h2 (now double-prefixed) leaves the <summary>References</summary> as the section's accessible name — matches the original design intent; net a11y neutral-to-positive.
Helpers are module-level stable fns; the components remain useMemo'd on contentId. Each element render runs 1-2 linear regex tests — negligible, no extra allocation vs the removed inline slicing.
The two-assistant-message seed yields two distinct content.id values, so each message's footnotes scope independently — the per-message-scoping test exercises real cross-message isolation.
No user-visible copy added or changed (References/Cited excerpt unchanged).

The click test asserts the inner details.footnote-quote opens, which depends on the 4-space-indented `> excerpt` attaching to footnote 1's <li> (multi-block footnote). If remark renders it as a top-level blockquote instead, the assertion is brittle. MUST verify at e2e runtime; if it does not attach, tighten the fixture markdown or scope the quote assertion to the target <li>.

`sup a` selector must resolve to the FORWARD footnote ref, not a backref (backrefs are returned null by the override so should not render). Confirm at e2e runtime that the click target is the superscript ref.
